claim: Browser extensions and third-party scripts have been caught intercepting ChatGPT conversations — including medical, financial, and code prompts — and reselling them as marketing data.
quote: Scripts designed to intercept conversations from leading AI platforms... were found in browser extensions, collecting data including medical questions, financial details, and proprietary code, sold for marketing analytics purposes.

claim: ChatGPT collects names, device fingerprints, and full conversation contents by default; staff and contractors can review chats for safety and training.
quote: OpenAI staff and authorized third-party contractors can review conversations for safety and model training.

claim: Italy's Garante fined OpenAI €15M in Dec 2024 for training ChatGPT on personal data scraped from the public web without a legal basis.
quote: The Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante) said OpenAI used personal data to train ChatGPT 'without having an adequate legal basis and violated the principle of transparency.'

claim: On 18 March 2026 the Court of Rome annulled the €15M Italian fine against OpenAI; the court has not yet published its full reasoning.
quote: A Rome court has cancelled a 15-million-euro ($17 million) fine that Italy's data protection authority imposed on ChatGPT maker OpenAI.

claim: Despite years of complaints, the Italy fine was the only successful GDPR enforcement against a generative-AI provider in Europe — and it has now been overturned.
quote: Generative AI and GDPR Enforcement in Europe: A Lot of Noise, One Fine, Zero Survivors.

claim: CNIL (France) and other EU DPAs accept 'legitimate interest' as a lawful basis for training LLMs on user content — provided opt-out and safeguards exist.
quote: European DPAs are more or less aligned on the position that digital service providers can rely on legitimate interest to use their user content for training LLMs, provided appropriate safeguards are in place.

claim: The EU Digital Omnibus, proposed in 2025, would explicitly let AI providers cite 'legitimate interest' as a GDPR basis if they offer an unconditional opt-out — but the Council may strip the provision before adoption.
quote: The European Commission has proposed amendments allowing AI providers to rely on legitimate interest as a legal basis for AI development.

claim: The EU AI Act's Article 10 obliges high-risk-system providers to use high-quality datasets, document data origin, and assess bias and gaps.
quote: High-risk AI systems must be developed using high-quality data sets for training, validation, and testing.

claim: From August 2026 every general-purpose AI provider in the EU must publish a 'sufficiently detailed summary' of training data using a mandatory Commission template.
quote: The template is a mandatory compliance tool that all general-purpose AI providers must use to publicly disclose high-level information about the data used to train their models.

claim: Anthropic agreed in Sept 2025 to pay $1.5B (~$3,000 per book × 500,000 books) to settle a class action over downloading 7M+ pirated books, including the Books3 dataset, to train Claude.
quote: Anthropic has agreed to pay $1.5 billion to settle a class-action lawsuit by book authors who claim the company took pirated copies of their works to train its chatbot.

claim: The judge in Bartz v. Anthropic ruled that training itself qualified as 'fair use', but acquiring the books from pirate sites did not — splitting the harvest from the use.
quote: A federal judge ruled in June that training AI chatbots on copyrighted books wasn't illegal but that Anthropic wrongfully acquired millions of books through pirate websites.

claim: In March 2025 a federal court approved an unconventional Clearview AI BIPA settlement: a 23% equity stake in the company (~$51.75M) shared with 100M+ Illinois class members.
quote: A federal judge in the Northern District of Illinois approved an unconventional class action settlement that grants class members a 23% equity stake in Clearview AI.

claim: Clearview AI's facial-recognition database holds 60+ billion images scraped from social media, news sites, Venmo and other public sources without consent.
quote: The company built a database of more than 60 billion facial images by scraping photographs from social media platforms, news websites, Venmo, and other publicly accessible online sources.

claim: In March 2023 Samsung engineers leaked semiconductor source code, defect-detection algorithms, and internal meeting notes to ChatGPT in three separate incidents within 20 days, prompting a company-wide ban.
quote: Within just 20 days of allowing ChatGPT use, engineers created AI chatbots data leaks with sensitive source code three separate times, forcing the tech giant to ban the AI tool company-wide.

claim: OmniGPT, a chatbot aggregator, was breached in 2025 and leaked 34M+ user messages plus thousands of API keys.
quote: OmniGPT, a widely used AI chatbot aggregator that connects users to multiple LLMs, suffered a major breach, exposing over 34 million user messages and thousands of API keys to the public.

claim: Netskope's 2026 Cloud and Threat Report finds 47% of workers using GenAI at work do so via personal accounts outside company oversight.
quote: According to Netskope's Cloud and Threat Report for 2026, nearly half (47%) of people using generative AI tools in the workplace are using personal accounts and applications to do so.

claim: Cyberhaven measured 73.8% of ChatGPT accounts in the workplace as personal, lacking enterprise security controls; LayerX reports 77% of GenAI users have pasted company data into a chatbot.
quote: 73.8% of ChatGPT accounts are non-corporate ones that lack the security and privacy controls of ChatGPT Enterprise.

claim: From 3 Nov 2025 LinkedIn turned on AI training using EU/EEA/Swiss/Canadian/HK profiles and public posts by default — opt-in for everyone, opt-out only via Settings → Data Privacy.
quote: LinkedIn now uses your data for AI by default, opt out now!

claim: LinkedIn's policy applies retroactively in some regions: anything posted before the cutover date is fair game for training even if you opt out afterwards.
quote: For users in regions where the policy takes effect on November 3, 2025, any information shared on the platform up to that date will be used for AI training, even if you opt out afterwards.

claim: In late July 2025, ~4,500 ChatGPT 'Share'-link conversations were found indexed by Google; researchers later estimated ~100,000 conversations had been scraped.
quote: Early checks found around 4,500 shared ChatGPT conversations appearing in Google results. Further research showed that roughly 100,000 conversations were scraped or archived by third parties.

claim: OpenAI killed the 'discoverable shared chats' feature on 5 Aug 2025 and started working with search engines to de-index leaked conversations.
quote: OpenAI does away with feature that made ChatGPT conversations discoverable by Google.

claim: The global data-broker market was ~$294B in 2025 and is forecast to hit $448B by 2031 (CAGR 7.27%); ~5,000 brokers operate worldwide.
quote: The data broker market size was valued at USD 294.27 billion in 2025 and estimated to grow from USD 315.66 billion in 2026 to reach USD 448.32 billion by 2031.

claim: 33 California-registered data brokers admitted in 2025 disclosures that they sell US-resident data to entities in China, Russia, North Korea or Iran; five include precise GPS location.
quote: 33 California-registered data brokers reported selling data to or sharing data with non-US actors in North Korea, China, Russia, and/or Iran, with five of these data brokers even reporting collecting precise geolocation data.

claim: California's DROP (Delete Request and Opt-Out Platform) launched 1 Jan 2026; from 1 Aug 2026 brokers must check it every 45 days and delete matched records.
quote: California launched the Delete Request and Opt-Out Platform (DROP) on January 1, 2026.

claim: Carlini et al. (2020) showed GPT-2 could be coaxed into spitting out hundreds of verbatim training examples — names, phone numbers, emails, code — by an outside attacker with only API access.
quote: We were able to extract hundreds of verbatim text sequences from the model's training data, including personally identifiable information (names, phone numbers, and email addresses), IRC conversations, code, and 128-bit UUIDs.

claim: A Nov 2025 arXiv paper ('Retracing the Past') shows production-aligned LLMs still leak memorized data when steered into high-entropy states by adversarial prompts.
quote: A new framework called Confusion-Inducing Attacks (CIA) systematically crafts adversarial prompts optimized to maximize token-level entropy, thereby steering the model towards a high-uncertainty state to extract memorized data.

claim: On free/Plus ChatGPT, training-on-your-chats is ON by default; opt-out is at Settings → Data Controls → 'Improve the model for everyone'.
quote: Navigate to Your Profile > Settings > Data Controls > Improve the model for everyone > Switch off the toggle.

claim: Shadow-AI-related breaches now make up ~20% of data-breach incidents and cost on average $4.63M each — about $670k more than the baseline breach.
quote: Shadow AI incidents now account for 20% of all breaches and carry a cost premium: $4.63 million versus $3.96 million for standard breaches.

claim: FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed in 2025 testimony that the FBI buys location histories on US citizens from commercial data brokers — bypassing the warrant process.
quote: FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed to Congress that the FBI is buying Americans' data from data brokers, including location histories, to track American citizens.
